name: Lamb Roast (Traditional Seder Dish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a few cuts in the roast so spices can be inserted into the cuts.
Deep cuts can also be made to facilitate serving.
2. Rub the leg of lamb all over with the spices: minced garlic, chopped fresh rosemary, vinegar, lemon juice, paprika, and black pepper.
3. Place the seasoned leg of lamb in a baking pan.
4. Mix the chicken soup with the remaining lemon juice and add about one-quarter of the quantity to the roast.
5. Roast the lamb in a preheated oven at 210°C (410°F) for 10 minutes, then turn the meat over.
6. Lower the heat to 150°C (300°F) and continue baking for about 25 minutes per kilogram of meat. Every 10 minutes, baste with the liquids and add some of the soup and lemon juice mixture.
7. Turn the roast occasionally during cooking.
The baking times are according to what is written in the recipe we have. As our oven does not have temperature settings (the oven is 25 years old and the writing on the knob has faded), we guessed the temperature, but it still turned out great. The dish was the best of the evening, though it looked somewhat overdone on the outside, it was as tender as butter.
